Canvas.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
A canvas for user interface elements. See <see cref="M:UI.StockCanvas" /> and <see cref="M:UI.AddCanvas" />.
</summary>
</doc>

Panel.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
A container for user interface elements. See <see cref="M:UI.Canvas.AddPanel" />.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform.documentation: A Unity engine Rect Transform for a UI object. See the Unity manual for more details.

UI.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Provides functionality for drawing and interacting with in-game user interface elements.
</summary>
<remarks>
For drawing 3D objects in the flight scene, see the Drawing service.
</remarks>
</doc>

AddCanvas.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add a new canvas.
</summary>
<remarks>
If you want to add UI elements to KSPs stock UI canvas, use <see cref="M:UI.StockCanvas" />.
</remarks>
</doc>

Button_get_Clicked.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the button has been clicked.
</summary>
<remarks>
This property is set to true when the user clicks the button.
A client script should reset the property to false in order to detect subsequent button presses.
</remarks>
</doc>

Button_get_RectTransform.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The rect transform for the text.
</summary>
</doc>

Button_get_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

Button_set_Clicked.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the button has been clicked.
</summary>
<remarks>
This property is set to true when the user clicks the button.
A client script should reset the property to false in order to detect subsequent button presses.
</remarks>
</doc>

Button_set_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

Canvas_AddButton.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add a button to the canvas.
</summary>
<param name="content">The label for the button.</param>
<param name="visible">Whether the button is visible.</param>
</doc>

Canvas_AddInputField.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add an input field to the canvas.
</summary>
<param name="visible">Whether the input field is visible.</param>
</doc>

Canvas_AddPanel.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Create a new container for user interface elements.
</summary>
<param name="visible">Whether the panel is visible.</param>
</doc>

Canvas_AddText.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add text to the canvas.
</summary>
<param name="content">The text.</param>
<param name="visible">Whether the text is visible.</param>
</doc>

Canvas_get_RectTransform.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The rect transform for the canvas.
</summary>
</doc>

Canvas_get_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

Canvas_set_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

Clear.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Remove all user interface elements.
</summary>
<param name="clientOnly">If true, only remove objects created by the calling client.</param>
</doc>

InputField_get_Changed.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the input field has been changed.
</summary>
<remarks>
This property is set to true when the user modifies the value of the input field.
A client script should reset the property to false in order to detect subsequent changes.
</remarks>
</doc>

InputField_get_RectTransform.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The rect transform for the input field.
</summary>
</doc>

InputField_get_Text.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The text component of the input field.
</summary>
<remarks>
Use <see cref="M:UI.InputField.Value" /> to get and set the value in the field.
This object can be used to alter the style of the input field's text.
</remarks>
</doc>

InputField_get_Value.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The value of the input field.
</summary>
</doc>

InputField_get_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

InputField_set_Changed.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the input field has been changed.
</summary>
<remarks>
This property is set to true when the user modifies the value of the input field.
A client script should reset the property to false in order to detect subsequent changes.
</remarks>
</doc>

InputField_set_Value.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The value of the input field.
</summary>
</doc>

InputField_set_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

Message.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Display a message on the screen.
</summary>
<remarks>
The message appears just like a stock message, for example quicksave or quickload messages.
</remarks>
<param name="content">Message content.</param>
<param name="duration">Duration before the message disappears, in seconds.</param>
<param name="position">Position to display the message.</param>
<param name="size">Size of the message, differs per position.</param>
<param name="color">The color of the message.</param>
</doc>

Panel_AddButton.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add a button to the panel.
</summary>
<param name="content">The label for the button.</param>
<param name="visible">Whether the button is visible.</param>
</doc>

Panel_AddInputField.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add an input field to the panel.
</summary>
<param name="visible">Whether the input field is visible.</param>
</doc>

Panel_AddPanel.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Create a panel within this panel.
</summary>
<param name="visible">Whether the new panel is visible.</param>
</doc>

Panel_AddText.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Add text to the panel.
</summary>
<param name="content">The text.</param>
<param name="visible">Whether the text is visible.</param>
</doc>

Panel_get_RectTransform.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>

</summary>
</doc>

Panel_get_Visible.documentation:

<summary>

</summary>
</doc>

Panel_set_Visible.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Whether the UI object is visible.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_AnchorMax.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The anchor point for the lower left corner of the rectangle defined as a fraction of the size of the parent rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_AnchorMin.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The anchor point for the upper right corner of the rectangle defined as a fraction of the size of the parent rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_LocalPosition.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles pivot point relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_LowerLeft.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles lower left corner relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_Pivot.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Location of the pivot point around which the rectangle rotates, defined as a fraction of the size of the rectangle itself.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_Position.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles pivot point relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_Rotation.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Rotation, as a quaternion, of the object around its pivot point.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_Scale.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Scale factor applied to the object in the x, y and z dimensions.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_Size.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Width and height of the rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_get_UpperRight.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles upper right corner relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Anchor.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Set the minimum and maximum anchor points as a fraction of the size of the parent rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_AnchorMax.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The anchor point for the lower left corner of the rectangle defined as a fraction of the size of the parent rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_AnchorMin.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
The anchor point for the upper right corner of the rectangle defined as a fraction of the size of the parent rectangle.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_LocalPosition.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles pivot point relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_LowerLeft.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles lower left corner relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Pivot.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Location of the pivot point around which the rectangle rotates, defined as a fraction of the size of the rectangle itself.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Position.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Position of the rectangles pivot point relative to the anchors.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Rotation.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Rotation, as a quaternion, of the object around its pivot point.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Scale.documentation:
<doc>
<summary>
Scale factor applied to the object in the x, y and z dimensions.
</summary>
</doc>

RectTransform_set_Size.documentation:

</summary>

RectTransform_set_UpperRight.documentation:

</doc>
